---
title: "Step 12: Simulate Use of Findings: Evaluation's Equivalent of a Dress Rehearsal"
description: "Detailed study notes for Step 12: Simulate Use of Findings: Evaluation's Equivalent of a Dress Rehearsal in Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation."
book_id: "essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Step"
step_number: 12
source_pages: "331-348"
citation_key: "patton_2012_essentials_utilization_focused_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step12-step-12-simulate-use-of-findings-evaluation-s-equivalent-of-a-dress-rehearsal:c0001"
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:utilization-focused-evaluation-checklist-17-steps-to-evaluations-that-are-useful:c0019"
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:utilization-focused-evaluation-checklist-17-steps-to-evaluations-that-are-useful:c0020"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2012</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Step 12</span><span>Source pages 331-348</span></div>

This note summarises step 12, "Simulate Use of Findings: Evaluation's Equivalent of a Dress Rehearsal," from Michael Quinn Patton's *Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation* [@patton_2012_essentials_utilization_focused_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a substitute for the book section.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source records at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the section's argument and procedures, and it does not reproduce textbook tables, checklists, exhibits, graphics, or extended passages. When a checklist, exhibit, or graphic is central, the note describes its function and leaves the source artifact in the evidence record.

## Core Argument

Simulating findings before data collection tests whether the design will produce information users can understand, own, and act on.

## Study Summary

Step 12 asks users to rehearse the future use of findings. By simulating possible results, interpretations, and decisions, users and evaluator can test whether the evaluation design is worth doing as planned.

The step is a final design check before data collection. It can reveal missing measures, unclear standards, implausible decisions, or weak ownership of the design.

Simulation also disciplines expectations. Users confront likely costs, uncertainties, and decision implications before evidence arrives, making later interpretation less reactive.

In the overall U-FE sequence, simulation connects methods negotiation to data gathering. It asks whether the chosen design can actually support intended use at the point of decision.

For close reading, track how this section uses simulation, intended use, primary intended users, and credibility while moving through finding scenarios, interpretation rehearsal, and decision rehearsal. The notes below identify what the section asks the evaluator to notice, what use problem it helps solve, and what should be checked against the saved evidence records before relying on the summary in applied work.

Within the wider book, this step belongs to the methods-and-data sequence: it negotiates credible evidence, tests use before data collection, and keeps fieldwork connected to utility.

## Key Concepts

- **simulation**: A rehearsal of likely findings, interpretations, and decisions before data collection to test utility and design fit.
- **intended use**: The specific way evaluation process or findings are expected to inform decisions, learning, action, or influence.
- **primary intended users**: Named people expected to use the evaluation and participate in major focus, design, interpretation, and use decisions.
- **credibility**: The degree to which intended users judge evaluation evidence, methods, and interpretation as trustworthy enough for their uses.
- **adaptive use**: Ongoing adjustment of evaluation questions, methods, communication, or follow-up as users, evidence, and context change.

## Section Structure

- Finding scenarios.
- Interpretation rehearsal.
- Decision rehearsal.
- Design revision.
- Go/no-go judgment.

## Practical Implications

- Run a findings-use simulation before data collection.
- Revise measures or design when simulated findings would not support action.
- Use simulation to test whether the evaluation is worth its expected cost.

## Concept Map

::: {.concept-map}
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Problem["Problem: What use problem does Step 12 help solve?"]
  Central["Concept: Simulate Use of Findings: Evaluation's Equivalent of a Dress Rehearsal"]
  Concept1["Concept: simulation"]
  Concept2["Concept: intended use"]
  Concept3["Concept: primary intended users"]
  Mechanism1["Mechanism: finding scenarios"]
  Mechanism2["Mechanism: interpretation rehearsal"]
  Mechanism3["Mechanism: decision rehearsal"]
  Implication1["Practice implication: Run a findings-use simulation before data collection."]
  Implication2["Practice implication: Revise measures or design when simulated findings would not support action."]
  Question["Open question: What would make this usable in a live evaluation?"]

  Problem --> Central
  Central --> Question
  Central --> Concept1
  Central --> Concept2
  Central --> Concept3
  Concept1 --> Mechanism1
  Concept2 --> Mechanism2
  Concept3 --> Mechanism3
  Mechanism1 --> Implication1
  Mechanism2 --> Implication2
  Implication1 --> Question

  classDef problem fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#b91c1c,color:#7f1d1d;
  classDef concept fill:#e6f3f1,stroke:#115e59,color:#134e4a;
  classDef mechanism fill:#e8f1ff,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#1e3a8a;
  classDef implication fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#be185d,color:#831843;
  class Problem problem;
  class Central concept;
  class Concept1,Concept2,Concept3 concept;
  class Mechanism1,Mechanism2,Mechanism3 mechanism;
  class Implication1,Implication2,Question implication;
```
:::

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Step 11: Make Sure Intended Users Understand Potential Methods Controversies and Their Implications](step-11-explain-methods-controversies-and-implications.qmd)
- Next: [Step 13: Gather Data With Ongoing Attention to Use](step-13-gather-data-with-ongoing-attention-to-use.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What use problem does step 12 help solve?
2. Which primary intended users would need to participate for this guidance to work?
3. How would this section change scoping, methods, interpretation, reporting, or follow-up?
4. Where could this guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## Source Records

<details class="source-chunks">
<summary>Source records</summary>
<ul class="source-chunk-list">
<li><code>chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step12-step-12-simulate-use-of-findings-evaluation-s-equivalent-of-a-dress-rehearsal:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:utilization-focused-evaluation-checklist-17-steps-to-evaluations-that-are-useful:c0019</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:utilization-focused-evaluation-checklist-17-steps-to-evaluations-that-are-useful:c0020</code></li>
</ul>
</details>

## References

- Patton, M. Q. (2012). *Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation*. SAGE Publications. Step 12: Simulate Use of Findings: Evaluation's Equivalent of a Dress Rehearsal, pages 331-348.
- Saved retrieval metadata: `data/research-library/evidence/essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation/step-evidence-summary.json`.
